topics.title: New zigzag snake bot that weaves toward food
topics.trigger: The project needed an additional bot personality to expand the roster of available snake opponents. A zigzag-style bot with a distinctive weaving movement pattern was contributed via pull request.
topics.response:
- `src/bots/zigzag-bot.ts` was created from scratch, implementing a scoring-based direction chooser. The bot evaluates each candidate move by simulating it, then scores it across several dimensions: food proximity via BFS path length, reachable area (used as a safety floor), wall proximity penalty, and a bonus for turning perpendicular to the current heading. The perpendicular-turn bonus is what produces the characteristic sawtooth weave.
- `src/bots/index.ts` was updated to import `zigzagBot` and append it to the `AVAILABLE_BOTS` array, making it selectable in the game alongside the existing nine bots.
topics.decisions:
- **Safety-first scoring with a hard floor**: Moves that leave the snake unable to reach its own tail are scored at a large negative value rather than being filtered out entirely. This preserves a fallback path when all moves are dangerous, letting the bot pick the least-bad option rather than returning null and crashing.
- **Perpendicular-turn bias over strict path-following**: Rather than building a dedicated zigzag planner, the bot adds a flat score bonus whenever the chosen direction is perpendicular to the current heading. This keeps the implementation simple and composable with the food-pursuit and safety logic, at the cost of the weave being opportunistic rather than geometrically precise.
- **Wall-margin penalty to protect the weave near edges**: Zigzagging close to a wall tends to trap the snake faster than it would a straight-moving bot, so a score penalty is applied when the next head position is within one cell of any border. This trades a small amount of aggressive food pursuit near edges for meaningfully better survival near boundaries.

recap: A new snake bot called Zigzag joined the roster of available opponents. It moves in a sawtooth weaving pattern, alternating left and right turns as it advances toward food, giving it a visually distinct style compared to the existing bots. The bot also applies a wall-avoidance penalty near the edges of the grid, where weaving movement is most likely to cause self-entrapment, and falls back gracefully when all available moves are risky rather than failing outright.
